sentences.text: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle.
For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead.
I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it.
Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist.
He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me.
The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised.
But overall, the reactions were positive.
I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be.
In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself.
I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: the psychological damage caused by being self-conscious about physical features
how peer pressure influences teenagers' decisions about their appearance
the importance of consulting professionals before making major life decisions
strategies for managing anxiety in social situations at school
gaining self-confidence by changing one's appearance through a new hairstyle
problems.explanation: 이마에 대한 걱정으로 같은 머리를 유지했지만, 헤어스타일 변화에 도전하여 자신감을 얻었다는 내용이 핵심이므로 '새로운 헤어스타일로 외모를 바꿔 자신감을 얻은 것'이 주제이다. ②는 심리적 피해, ③은 또래 압력, ④는 전문가 상담의 중요성, ⑤는 불안 관리 전략으로 본문 핵심과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: Physical appearance is the most important factor in building self-confidence.
Stepping out of one's comfort zone can lead to unexpected boosts in self-confidence.
People should always follow the advice of professional stylists for personal changes.
The opinions of classmates are the best measure of whether a change was worthwhile.
Avoiding attention to one's weaknesses is the safest way to maintain confidence.
problems.explanation: 안전지대를 벗어나 헤어스타일을 바꾼 것이 예상치 못한 자신감 향상으로 이어졌다는 것이 요지이다. ①은 외모가 가장 중요하다는 과장, ③은 전문가 조언 추종, ④는 급우 의견이 최선의 척도, ⑤는 약점 회피가 안전하다는 반대 메시지이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: Why Teenagers Should Never Change Their Appearance
Hair Salon Secrets: Professional Tips for the Perfect Look
Peer Reactions: How Classmates Shape Our Identity
The Science Behind Self-Consciousness and Physical Features
A New Hairstyle, A New Me: The Power of Small Changes
problems.explanation: 헤어스타일이라는 작은 변화가 자기 인식에 큰 영향을 미쳤다는 글의 전체 메시지를 포괄한다. ②는 외모 변화 반대, ③은 미용실 비법, ④는 또래 반응이 정체성을 형성, ⑤는 자의식의 과학으로 제목에 부적절하다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: <u>Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous</u>: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: The writer had no interest in completing the third challenge at all.
The writer's nervousness was caused by a lack of information about hair salons.
The third challenge was physically the most demanding of all three challenges.
Changing the hairstyle was emotionally the most daunting of the three challenges for the writer.
The writer was nervous because classmates had warned against changing hairstyles.
problems.explanation: '모든 도전 중 세 번째가 가장 긴장되게 만들었다: 헤어스타일 변경'이므로, 헤어스타일 바꾸기가 감정적으로 세 가지 중 가장 부담스러웠다는 의미이다. ①은 관심 없음, ③은 미용실 정보 부족, ④는 물리적으로 힘듦, ⑤는 급우들의 경고로 본문에 없는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. <u>I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be.</u> In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: The actual process of changing appearance turned out to be less challenging than the writer had feared.
The writer found that cutting hair was technically simple and required no professional help.
The writer concluded that appearance changes are always easy for everyone.
The difficulty of the hairstyle change was exactly what the writer had predicted.
The writer believed the positive reactions were insincere and exaggerated.
problems.explanation: '다른 외모를 시도하는 것이 생각했던 만큼 어렵지 않았다'는 외모 변화의 실제 과정이 사전 우려보다 덜 힘들었다는 의미이다. ②는 전문가 도움 불필요, ③은 모든 사람에게 항상 쉽다는 일반화, ④는 예측과 정확히 같았다는 반대, ⑤는 반응이 진심이 아니었다는 것으로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. <u>I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.</u>
choices: The writer was surprised by how much the experience improved self-perception.
The writer had always known that leaving the comfort zone would improve self-esteem.
The writer regretted stepping out of the comfort zone because the effect was too strong.
The writer expected the comfort zone challenge to have no effect whatsoever.
The positive effect was limited to how others perceived the writer, not self-perception.
problems.explanation: '안전지대를 벗어나는 것이 자기 자신에 대한 감정에 이렇게 긍정적인 영향을 미칠 줄 몰랐다'는 경험이 자기 인식을 얼마나 개선했는지에 놀랐다는 의미이다. ①은 항상 알고 있었다, ③은 후회, ④는 효과가 전혀 없을 것으로 예상, ⑤는 타인의 인식에만 제한으로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분으로부터 추론할 수 있는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. <u>I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it.</u> Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: The writer's forehead was objectively larger than average according to medical standards.
The writer had received frequent comments from others about the size of the forehead.
The writer used the hairstyle as a way to conceal a perceived physical insecurity.
The writer wanted a new hairstyle but could not afford to visit a hair salon.
The writer's concern about the forehead was based on a doctor's advice.
problems.explanation: 이마가 크다고 생각해서 주목받고 싶지 않았다는 것에서, 같은 헤어스타일을 유지한 것이 신체적 콤플렉스를 가리기 위한 수단이었음을 추론할 수 있다. ①은 객관적으로 큼, ③은 다른 사람의 잦은 언급, ④는 경제적 이유, ⑤는 의사 조언으로 본문에서 추론할 수 없다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most ________________: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
problems.explanation: 이마에 대한 걱정 때문에 헤어스타일을 바꾸는 것이 가장 '긴장되게(nervous)' 만들었다. excited(신남), bored(지루함), relieved(안도), curious(궁금함)는 이어지는 걱정의 맥락과 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to ________________ to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
problems.explanation: 이마가 크다고 생각해서 '관심을 끌고 싶지 않았다(draw attention to)'가 문맥상 적절. pay a visit(방문), make reference(언급), lend support(지원), give credit(인정)은 문맥에 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and _________________________________________________. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
problems.blankTarget: helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me
choices: suggested I keep the same hairstyle to avoid any risk
helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me
recommended a completely different hair color instead
told me that my concerns were unreasonable and unnecessary
advised me to consult a dermatologist before making changes
problems.explanation: 글쓴이의 걱정을 듣고 '자신에게 어울리는 짧은 헤어스타일을 찾도록 도와주었다'가 원문 내용이다. ①은 같은 스타일 유지 권유, ③은 머리 색 변경, ④는 걱정이 불합리하다는 반응, ⑤는 피부과 상담 권유로 문맥과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were ________________. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
problems.explanation: 급우들이 놀랐지만 '전반적으로 반응은 긍정적이었다(positive)'. hostile(적대적), indifferent(무관심), critical(비판적), confused(혼란스러운)는 But overall이 이끄는 긍정적 전환과 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on _______________________.
choices: how my classmates performed in school
whether the stylist would receive more customers
how quickly my hair would grow back
whether the school would change its dress code
problems.explanation: '자신에 대해 어떻게 느끼는지(how I felt about myself)'에 긍정적 영향을 미칠 줄 몰랐다가 원문. ①은 급우 성적, ③은 미용사 고객, ④는 머리 자라는 속도, ⑤는 학교 복장 규정으로 문맥과 무관하다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me.
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. ( ① ) For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. ( ② ) I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. ( ③ ) Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 미용실에 가서 스타일리스트와 이야기한 직후에, 그가 걱정을 듣고 어울리는 헤어스타일을 찾아주었다는 내용이 이어져야 자연스럽다. ④ 뒤에 위치하면 '미용실 방문 → 상담 → 스타일 결정'의 흐름이 된다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself.
problems.passage: The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. ( ① ) I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. ( ② ) I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself. ( ③ )
problems.explanation: '다른 외모가 생각보다 어렵지 않았다'는 깨달음 뒤에, '사실 더 자신감을 갖게 해주었다'는 심화된 긍정 반응이 와야 자연스럽다. In fact은 앞 내용을 강화하므로 ② 위치가 적절.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me.

chunks.A: But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be.
chunks.B: The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised.
chunks.C: In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself.

problems.explanation: (B) 다음 날 학교에서 급우들 놀람 → (A) But으로 전환, 전반적 반응 긍정 + 생각보다 어렵지 않았다는 깨달음 → (C) In fact으로 강화, 자신감 향상. 시간 순서와 논리적 심화가 자연스럽다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: The writer had changed hairstyles frequently before this challenge.
The stylist recommended that the writer keep the same hairstyle.
The writer's classmates reacted negatively to the new hairstyle.
The writer felt less confident after changing the hairstyle.
The writer had kept the same hairstyle for many years due to concern about the forehead.
problems.explanation: ②번 문장: 이마가 걱정되어 수년간 같은 헤어스타일을 유지했다. ①은 자주 바꿨다는 반대, ②는 같은 스타일 유지 권유가 아님, ③은 반응이 긍정적이었음, ⑤는 더 자신감을 얻었으므로 틀림.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: The third challenge made the writer more nervous than the other two.
The classmates were completely unsurprised by the writer's new look.
The writer went to a hair salon after gaining confidence from previous challenges.
The stylist helped the writer choose a suitable shorter hairstyle.
The experience had a positive effect on the writer's self-confidence.
problems.explanation: ⑥번 문장에서 급우들이 '조금 놀랐다(were a bit surprised)'고 했으므로, '전혀 놀라지 않았다(completely unsurprised)'는 일치하지 않는다. ①②③⑤는 모두 본문과 일치한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: The writer chose changing a hairstyle as the third personal challenge.
The writer's concern about the forehead led to keeping the same hairstyle.
The overall reactions from classmates to the new hairstyle were positive.
The writer felt more confident about self-image after the hairstyle change.
The writer visited the hair salon before attempting the first two challenges.
problems.explanation: ④번 문장에서 '첫 두 도전 후(after the first two challenges)' 자신감이 생겨 미용실에 갔다고 했으므로, '첫 두 도전 전에 미용실에 갔다'는 일치하지 않는다. ①②④⑤는 모두 본문과 일치한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
problems.summaryTemplate: The writer overcame a long-held ____(A)____ about appearance by changing hairstyles, which unexpectedly boosted ____(B)____.
problems.explanation: 오랫동안 지닌 외모에 대한 불안감(insecurity)을 극복하고 자신감(self-confidence)이 향상되었다. ①의 curiosity와 academic performance, ③의 ambition과 popularity, ④의 habit과 creativity, ⑤의 social status는 본문 내용과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
problems.summaryTemplate: By ____(A)____ a new hairstyle, the writer learned that leaving one's comfort zone can positively ____(B)____ self-perception.
problems.explanation: 새 헤어스타일을 시도(trying)함으로써 안전지대를 벗어나면 자기 인식을 긍정적으로 변화시킬 수 있다(transform)는 것을 배웠다. ①의 rejecting과 damage, ③의 avoiding과 maintain, ④의 criticizing과 complicate, ⑤의 designing과 replace는 본문과 무관하다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 it이 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought <u>it</u> was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: the hairstyle the writer had kept for many years
the hair salon the writer eventually visited
problems.explanation: 앞 문장에서 이마(forehead)가 걱정되었다고 했으므로, it은 '글쓴이의 이마'를 가리킨다. 이마가 크다고 생각했고 주목받고 싶지 않았다는 문맥과 일치. ①은 헤어스타일, ③은 도전, ④는 미용실, ⑤는 전체 외모이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 it이 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous: changing my hairstyle. For many years, I had kept the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I had always thought <u>it</u> was large, and I didn't want to draw attention to it. Since I was feeling confident after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon and talked to the stylist. He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me. The next day at school, my classmates were a bit surprised. But overall, the reactions were positive. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be. In fact, it made me feel more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
choices: the reaction from the writer's classmates
the process of going to the hair salon
the experience of trying a different look
the writer's concern about the forehead
problems.explanation: 앞 문장에서 '다른 외모를 시도하는 것(trying a different look)'이 어렵지 않았다고 했으므로, it은 '다른 외모를 시도한 경험'을 가리킨다. ①은 급우 반응, ②는 미용실 방문 과정, ④는 스타일리스트 조언, ⑤는 이마 걱정이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one ①<u>made</u> me the most nervous. For many years, I ②<u>had kept</u> the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead. I didn't want to draw attention ③<u>to</u> it. He helped me ④<u>finding</u> a shorter hairstyle that suited me. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I ⑤<u>thought</u> it would be.
problems.explanation: help + 목적어 + (to) 동사원형이므로 finding → find. ① make + O + 형용사(5형식) ✓ ② 과거완료(대과거) ✓ ③ draw attention to ✓ ⑤ 시제 일치(thought) ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: Of all the challenges, the third one made me ①<u>the most nervous</u>. Since I was feeling ②<u>confident</u> after the first two challenges, I went to a hair salon. He helped me find a shorter hairstyle ③<u>that suited</u> me. It made me ④<u>to feel</u> more confident about myself. I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone ⑤<u>would have</u> such a positive effect.
problems.explanation: make + 목적어 + 동사원형(사역동사)이므로 to feel → feel. ① the most + 형용사(최상급) ✓ ② 감정형용사 confident ✓ ③ 주격관계대명사 that + 동사 ✓ ⑤ expect that ~ would ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: For many years, I ①<u>had kept</u> the same hairstyle because I had been ②<u>worried</u> about my forehead. I had always thought it was large. I realized that trying a different look wasn't as ③<u>difficultly</u> as I thought. In fact, it made me feel more ④<u>confident</u> about myself. I didn't expect that ⑤<u>stepping</u> out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect.
problems.explanation: as ~ as 비교 구문에서 be동사 뒤에는 형용사가 와야 한다. difficultly(부사) → difficult(형용사). ① 과거완료 ✓ ② worried(감정 과거분사) ✓ ④ 형용사 confident ✓ ⑤ 동명사 주어 stepping ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 that의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: I realized <u>that</u> trying a different look wasn't as difficult.
I didn't expect <u>that</u> stepping out of my comfort zone would help.
She knew <u>that</u> the writer was nervous about the change.
He found a shorter hairstyle <u>that</u> suited me.
I believed <u>that</u> the reactions would be negative.
problems.explanation: ④의 that은 주격 관계대명사(a shorter hairstyle을 수식). ①②③⑤는 모두 명사절을 이끄는 접속사 that이다. ④만 관계대명사로 쓰임이 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 make의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: The third one <u>made</u> me the most nervous.
She <u>made</u> a beautiful dress for the party.
It <u>made</u> me feel more confident about myself.
The news <u>made</u> him reconsider his decision.
problems.explanation: ④의 made는 '만들다(제작)'의 의미로 3형식(make + 목적어). ①②③⑤는 모두 사역동사 make(make + O + 형용사/V원형: ~하게 만들다)로 5형식이다. ④만 쓰임이 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 as의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: It wasn't <u>as</u> difficult as I thought it would be.
She is not <u>as</u> tall as her brother.
The test was <u>as</u> easy as the teacher had promised.
The water wasn't <u>as</u> bad as I had thought.
<u>As</u> I was tired, I went to bed early.
problems.explanation: ④의 as는 이유의 접속사('~이므로'). ①②③⑤는 모두 동등비교(as ~ as)의 as이다. ④만 쓰임이 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the more nervous.
problems.explanation: Of all ~(셋 이상 비교)이므로 최상급 the most를 써야 한다. more는 비교급으로 둘을 비교할 때 쓴다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: I had always thought it was large, and I didn't want to draw attention at it.
problems.explanation: draw attention to = '~에 관심을 끌다'. draw attention은 전치사 to와 함께 쓴다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: He listened to my concerns and helped me finding a shorter hairstyle that suited me.
problems.explanation: help + 목적어 + (to) 동사원형. help 뒤에는 원형부정사가 온다. finding(동명사)은 불가.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: He helped me find a shorter hairstyle who suited me.
problems.explanation: 선행사가 사물(hairstyle)이므로 관계대명사 that 또는 which를 써야 한다. who는 사람을 선행사로 취하는 관계대명사이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: In fact, it made me to feel more confident about myself.
problems.explanation: 사역동사 make + 목적어 + 동사원형. make는 to부정사가 아닌 원형부정사를 목적격 보어로 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: I didn't expect that to step out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
problems.explanation: that절의 주어 자리에는 동명사가 적절하다. 'stepping out of my comfort zone would have ~'에서 stepping이 동명사 주어이다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: 내가 스스로 선택한 모든 도전 중에서, 세 번째가 나를 가장 긴장하게 만들었다.
problems.answer: Of all the challenges I chose for myself, the third one made me the most nervous.
problems.explanation: Of all ~(최상급 구문) / the third one(주어) / made me the most nervous(make + O + 형용사 최상급)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: 그는 나의 걱정을 듣고 나에게 어울리는 더 짧은 헤어스타일을 찾도록 도와주었다.
problems.answer: He listened to my concerns and helped me find a shorter hairstyle that suited me.
problems.explanation: He listened to my concerns(걱정을 들었다) / and helped me find(help + O + V원형) / a shorter hairstyle that suited me(관계대명사 that 수식)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: 나는 안전지대를 벗어나는 것이 내 자신에 대한 감정에 그렇게 긍정적인 영향을 미칠 줄 몰랐다.
problems.answer: I didn't expect that stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on how I felt about myself.
problems.explanation: I didn't expect that(~을 예상하지 못했다) / stepping out of my comfort zone(동명사 주어) / would have such a positive effect on(~에 영향을 미치다) / how I felt about myself(간접의문문)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: 나는 다른 외모를 시도하는 것이 생각했던 것만큼 어렵지 않다는 것을 깨달았다.
problems.answer: I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult as I thought it would be.
problems.explanation: I realized that(~라는 것을 깨달았다) / trying a different look(동명사 주어) / wasn't as difficult as(동등비교 부정) / I thought it would be(생각했던 것만큼)

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Of all the challenges, the third one made me the _______ (nervous).
problems.explanation: Of all ~(셋 이상 비교)이므로 최상급 the most nervous가 적절. make + O + 형용사 구문.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: For many years, I _______ (keep) the same hairstyle because I had been worried about my forehead.
problems.explanation: 과거 시점(도전 당시)보다 더 이전에 수년간 유지해 온 것이므로 과거완료 had kept이 적절.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: He listened to my concerns and helped me _______ (find) a shorter hairstyle that suited me.
problems.explanation: help + 목적어 + (to) 동사원형. help는 원형부정사를 허용하므로 find가 적절.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: In fact, it made me _______ (feel) more confident about myself.
problems.explanation: 사역동사 make + 목적어 + 동사원형. make는 원형부정사를 목적격 보어로 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: I didn't expect that _______ (step) out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect.
problems.explanation: that절의 주어 자리에 동명사 stepping이 와야 한다. '안전지대를 벗어나는 것이 ~할 줄 몰랐다'.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Stepping out of my comfort zone would have such a positive effect on _______ I felt about myself.
problems.explanation: '내가 자신에 대해 어떻게 느꼈는지'이므로 간접의문문을 이끄는 how가 적절. what(무엇), which(어떤), that(접속사), where(어디)는 문맥에 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: I realized that trying a different look wasn't as difficult _______ I thought it would be.
problems.explanation: not as ~ as 동등비교 구문. '~만큼 ~하지 않은'의 의미로 as가 적절. than은 비교급과 쓰이고, that/like/so는 이 구문에 맞지 않는다.
